name: (R)-3-hydroxybutyrate
description: The conjugate base of (R)-3-hydroxybutyric acid.

name: 2-hydroxyestrone
description: A 2-hydroxy steroid that is estrone substituted by a hydroxy group at position 2.

name: oleamide
description: A fatty amide derived from oleic acid.

name: N-carbamoyl-beta-alaninate
description: A monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of N-carbamoyl-β-alanine arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: 2'-deoxynucleoside 3'-monophosphate(2-)
description: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any 2'-deoxynucleoside 3'-monophosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

name: coproporphyrin III(4-)
description: A cyclic tetrapyrrole anion obtained by deprotonation of the four carboxy groups of coproporphyrin III; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (14S,15R)-EET(1-)
description: A 14,15-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (14S,15R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (14R,15S)-EET(1-)
description: A 14,15-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (14R,15S)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (11S,12R)-EET(1-)
description: An 11,12-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (11S,12R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (11R,12S)-EET(1-)
description: An 11,12-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (11R,12S)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (8S,9R)-EET(1-)
description: An 8,9-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (8S,9R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: tauroursodeoxycholate
description: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of tauroursodeoxycholic acid arising from deprotonation of the sulfonate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: glycoursodeoxycholate
description: A N-acylglycinate that is the conjugate base of glycoursodeoxycholic acid. obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-tetracosanoyltaurine(1-)
description: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-tetracosanoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 18(S)-HEPE(1-)
description: An 18-HEPE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 18(S)-HEPE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 15(S)-HEPE(1-)
description: A HEPE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 15(S)-HEPE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: O(3)-(N-acetyl-beta-D-galactosaminyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucuronosyl-(1->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-xylosyl)-L-serine(1-) residue
description: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the glucuronosyl carboxy group of O3-(N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-β-D-glucuronosyl-(1→3)-β-D-galactosyl-(1→3)-β-D-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-D-xylosyl)-L-serine residue.

name: pregnenolone sulfate(1-)
description: A steroid sulfate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of pregnenolone sulfate, obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: lithocholate sulfate(2-)
description: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxylic acid and sulfate functions of lithocholic acid sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-oleoylglycinate
description: An N-acylglycinate resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoylglycine. It is believed to be an intermediate in oleamide biosynthesis. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-oleoyl-L-phenylalaninate
description: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-phenylalanine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-arachidonoyl-L-phenylalaninate
description: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-arachidonoyl-L-phenylalanine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-oleoyl-L-serinate
description: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-serine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-oleoyl-L-glutaminate
description: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-glutamine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-oleoyl-L-leucinate
description: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-leucine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: 14,15-epoxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z)-icosatrienoyl-CoA(4-)
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 14,15-epoxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z)-icosatrienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5-hydroxy-(6E,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 5-hydroxy-(6E,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 12-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,10E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 12-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,10E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 15-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 15-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5'-end 2'-deoxyribonucleoside residue
description: A nucleoside residue derived from any 2'-deoxyribonucleoside having the linkage point at position 3'.

name: (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3-sulfate(1-)
description: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3-sulfate.

name: (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3,24-disulfate(2-)
description: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo groups of (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3,24-disulfate.

name: 13(R)-HODE(1-)
description: A 13-HODE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 13(R)-HODE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 19-hydroxy-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
description: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional hydroxy susbstituent at position 19.

name: 19-oxo-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
description: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional oxo susbstituent at position 19.

name: acetoacetate
description: A 3-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of acetoacetic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: 17beta-hydroxy-5alpha-estr-1(10)-en-3-one
description: A 3-oxo-5α-steroid that is 5α-estrane which is substituted by an oxo group at position 3, a hydroxy group at the 17β position, and which has a double bond between positions 1 and 10. It is a 19-norsteroid arising from successive oxidation of dihydrotestosterone at C19.

name: myristamide
description: A fatty amide of myristic acid.

name: 2-[(11R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,12E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (11R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,12E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl.

name: 2-[(15R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (15R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl.

name: 2-[(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl.

name: (25R)-26-hydroxycholestanol
description: A cholestanoid that is cholestanol which is substituted by a hydroxy group at position 26 and has R-configuration at position 25.

name: arachidonoyl amine
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of arachidonic acid with ammonia.

name: N(6)-[(E)-but-2-enoyl]-L-lysine residue
description: An N6-acyl-L-lysine residue derived from N6-[(E)-but-2-enoyl]-L-lysine.

name: (5Z,8S,9S,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8S,9S,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (5Z,8Z,11Z,14R,15R)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,8Z,11Z)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8Z,11Z,14R,15R)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (5Z,8Z,11R,12R,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,8Z,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8Z,11R,12R,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (5Z,8Z,11S,12S,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,8Z,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8Z,11S,12S,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (5Z,8Z,11Z,14S,15S)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,8Z,11Z)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8Z,11Z,14S,15S)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (5Z,8R,9R,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
description: A (5Z,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8R,9R,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the phosphate to the amino group of 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-15(S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the phosphate to the amino group of 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3- phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl.

name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3- phosphocholine
description: A prostanoid-LPC that is the ammonium betaine of a 2-(prostaglandin H2)-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine arising from deprotonation of the phosphate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 1,2-diheptanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A phosphatidylcholine 14:0 in which both acyl groups are specified as heptanoyl.

name: 2-heptanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as heptanoyl.

name: O(3)-(beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->4)-beta-D-Xyl)-L-serine(2-) residue
description: An α-amino-acid residue anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups of O3-(β-D-GlcA-(1→3)-β-D-GalNAc-(1→4)-β-D-GlcA-(1→3)-β-D-Gal-(1→3)-β-D-Gal-(1→4)-β-D-Xyl)-L-serine residue.

name: O(3)-(beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->4)-beta-D-Xyl)-L-serine(2-) residue
description: An α-amino-acid residue anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups of O3-(β-D-GalNAc-(1→4)-β-D-GlcA-(1→3)-β-D-GalNAc-(1→4)-β-D-GlcA-(1→3)-β-D-Gal-(1→3)-β-D-Gal-(1→4)-β-D-Xyl)-L-serine residue

name: all-trans-retinyl heptanoate
description: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of heptanoic acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

name: (S,S)-bis-(3-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate(1-)
description: A 3,3'-lysobisphosphatidate obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of (S,S)-bis-(3-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 1-(4-methoxyphenyl)-N-methyl-N-[(3-methyloxetan-3-yl)methyl]methanamine(1+)
description: An ammonium ion obtained by protonation of the amino group of 1-(4-methoxyphenyl)-N-methyl-N-[(3-methyloxetan-3-yl)methyl]methanamine. The major species at pH 7.3.

name: 2-{[(4-methoxybenzyl)(methyl)amino]methyl}-2-methylpropane-1,3-diol(1+)
description: An ammonium ion resulting from the protonation of the amino group of 2-{[(4-methoxybenzyl)(methyl)amino]methyl}-2-methylpropane-1,3-diol.

name: [8)-alpha-Neu5Ac-(2->]n polyanion
description: A polyanionic polymer obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups of [8)-α-Neu5Ac-(2→]n; major species at pH 7.3.

name: all-trans-retinyl octanoate
description: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of octanoic acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

name: 2-dodecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as dodecanoyl.

name: all-trans-retinyl dodecanoate
description: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of dodecanoic (lauric) acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

name: alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->8)-alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->3)-beta-D-galactoside(2-)
description: An N-acetylneuraminate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups of any α-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2→8)-α-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2→3)-β-D-galactoside; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 4-nitrophenyl sulfate
description: An aryl sulfate oxoanion resulting from the deprotonation of the sulfooxy group of 4-nitrophenyl hydrogen sulfate. The major microspecies at pH 7.3.

name: O-acetyl-L-serine residue
description: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from O-acetyl-L-serine.

name: (5S)-5-hydroxy-L-lysinium residue
description: An L-lysinium residue that is substituted by an hydroxy group at position 5 (the 5S stereoisomer); major microspecies at pH 7.3.

name: (9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadecatrienamide
description: A fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of α-linolenic acid with ammonia.

name: 2'-3'-cGAMP(2-)
description: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 2'-3'-cGAMP; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatrienamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of all-cis-icosa-8,11,14-trienoic acid with ammonia.

name: (11Z,14Z,17Z)-icosatrienamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of all-cis-icosa-11,14,17-trienoic acid with ammonia.

name: (11Z,14Z)-icosadienamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoic acid with ammonia.

name: nervonamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (15Z)-tetracosenoic acid with ammonia.

name: gondamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (11Z)-icos-11-enoic acid with ammonia.

name: petroselinamide
description: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of petroselinic acid with ammonia.

name: N-(docosanoyl)ethanolamine
description: An N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine that is the ethanolamide of docosanoic acid.

name: N-(15Z-tetracosenoyl)ethanolamine
description: A N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine that is the ethanolamide of (15Z)-tetracosenoic acid.

name: N-oleoyltaurine(1-)
description: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-oleoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-docosanoyltaurine(1-)
description: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate(1−) obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of N-docosanoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-nervonoyltaurine(1-)
description: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-nervonoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: angiotensin I dizwitterion
description: A peptide zwitterion that is the dizwitterionic form of angiotensin I having both carboxy groups deprotonated and the aspartyl amino group and arginine side-chain protonated. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

name: [des-Phe(8), des-Arg(9)]-bradykinin(1+)
description: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group and protonation of the primary amino and guanidino groups of [des-Phe<small><sup>8</small></sup>, des-Arg<small><sup>9</small></sup>]-bradykinin. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

name: neurotensin(1+)
description: A peptide cation obtained from the deprotonation of the carboxy groups of <small>L</small>-α-glutamyl and <small>L</small>-leucine residues, and protonation of the side chains of <small>L</small>-lysyl and <small>L</small>-arginyl residues of neurotensin. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

name: S-nitroso-L-cysteine residue
description: An L-α-amino-acid residue derived from S-nitroso-L-cysteine.

name: N-arachidonoyl-L-serine(1-)
description: A N-(fatty acyl)-L-serine(1−) that is the conjugate base of N-arachidonoyl-L-serine, resulting from the derotonation of the carboxy group. Major species at pH 7.3.

name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
description: A 1-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the amino to the phosphate group of 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine.

name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
description: A phosphatidylethanolamine 37:5 zwitterion in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as 10Z-heptadecenoyl and arachidonoyl respectively.

name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
description: A phosphatidylethanolamine 35:3 zwitterion in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as 10Z-heptadecenoyl and linoleoyl respectively.

name: retinal
description: An enal that consists of 3,7-dimethyl-9-nona-2,4,6,8-tetraenal (double bond geometry unspecified) carrying a 2,6,6-trimethylcyclohex-1-en-1-yl group at the 9-position.

name: choline
description: A choline that is the parent compound of the cholines class, consisting of ethanolamine having three methyl substituents attached to the amino function.

name: acetylcholine
description: Actylcholine is an ester of acetic acid and choline, which acts as a neurotransmitter.

name: pyruvate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of pyruvic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: water
description: An oxygen hydride consisting of an oxygen atom that is covalently bonded to two hydrogen atoms

name: hydron
description: The general name for the hydrogen nucleus, to be used without regard to the hydrogen nuclear mass (either for hydrogen in its natural abundance or where it is not desired to distinguish between the isotopes).

name: D-threo-isocitrate(3-)
description: The D-threo-form of isocitrate(3−).

name: (R)-malate(2-)
description: An optically active form of malate having (R)-configuration.

name: (S)-malate(2-)
description: An optically active form of malate having (S)-configuration.

name: formate
description: A monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of formic acid. Induces severe metabolic acidosis and ocular injury in human subjects.

name: malonate(2-)
description: A dicarboxylic acid dianion obtained by the deprotonation of the carboxy groups of malonic acid.

name: (R)-2-hydroxyglutarate(2-)
description: A 2-hydroxyglutarate(2−) that has (2R)-configuration.

name: 5,6-dihydrouracil
description: A pyrimidine obtained by formal addition of hydrogen across the 5,6-position of uracil.

name: 5alpha-androstane-3,17-dione
description: The 5α-stereoisomer of androstane-3,17-dione.

name: dihydroxyacetone
description: A ketotriose consisting of acetone bearing hydroxy substituents at positions 1 and 3. The simplest member of the class of ketoses and the parent of the class of glycerones.

name: androsterone
description: An androstanoid that is 5α-androstane having a hydroxy substituent at the 3α-position and an oxo group at the 17-position. It is a metabolite of dehydroepiandrosterone .

name: halide anion
description: A monoatomic monoanion resulting from the addition of an electron to any halogen atom.

name: 5beta-cholestan-3-one
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestane substituted by an oxo group at position 3.

name: cholesterol
description: A cholestanoid consisting of cholestane having a double bond at the 5,6-position as well as a 3β-hydroxy group.

name: cholest-4-en-3-one
description: A cholestanoid that is cholest-4-ene substituted by an oxo group at position 3.

name: guanine
description: A 2-aminopurine carrying a 6-oxo substituent.

name: hydrogen peroxide
description: An inorganic peroxide consisting of two hydroxy groups joined by a covalent oxygen-oxygen single bond.

name: 17beta-hydroxy-5alpha-androstan-3-one
description: A 17β-hydroxy steroid that is testosterone in which the 4,5 double bond has been reduced to a single bond with α-configuration at position 5.

name: (R)-carnitine
description: The (R)-enantiomer of carnitine.

name: ubiquinones
description: Any benzoquinone derived from 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone; one of a group of naturally occurring homologues. The redox-active quinoid moiety usually carries a polyprenoid side chain at position 6, the number of isoprenoid units in which is species-specific. Ubiquinones are involved in the control of mitochondrial electron transport, and are also potent anti-oxidants.

name: androst-4-ene-3,17-dione
description: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is androst-4-ene substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 17. It is a steroid hormone synthesized in the adrenal glands and gonads.

name: phenylacetaldehyde
description: An aldehyde that consists of acetaldehyde bearing a methyl substituent; the parent member of the phenylacetaldehyde class of compounds.

name: oxaloacetate(2-)
description: A C4-dicarboxylate resuting from deprotonation of both carboxy groups of oxaloacetic acid.

name: 17beta-estradiol
description: The 17β-isomer of estradiol.

name: lanosterol
description: A tetracyclic triterpenoid that is lanosta-8,24-diene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at the 3β position. It is the compound from which all steroids are derived.

name: carbon dioxide
description: A one-carbon compound with formula CO<small><sub>2</sub></small> in which the carbon is attached to each oxygen atom by a double bond. A colourless, odourless gas under normal conditions, it is produced during respiration by all animals, fungi and microorganisms that depend directly or indirectly on living or decaying plants for food.

name: alpha,alpha-trehalose
description: A trehalose in which both glucose residues have α-configuration at the anomeric carbon.

name: pregnenolone
description: A 20-oxo steroid that is pregn-5-ene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 20.

name: 5alpha-cholest-8-en-3beta-ol
description: A cholestanoid that is 5α-cholestane substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3.

name: (S)-lactate
description: An optically active form of lactate having (S)-configuration.

name: 4-methylthio-2-oxobutanoate
description: The 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion derived from 4-methylthio-2-oxobutanoic acid. The major microspecies at pH 7.3, it is formed from <small>L</small>-methionine via the action of methionine transaminase.

name: 2-oxobutanoate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxobutanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: (S)-2-hydroxyglutarate(2-)
description: A 2-hydroxyglutarate(2−) that has (2S)-configuration.

name: 2-oxoglutarate(2-)
description: An oxo dicarboxylate obtained by deprotonation of both carboxy groups of 2-oxoglutaric acid.

name: corticosterone
description: A 21-hydroxy steroid that consists of pregn-4-ene substituted by hydroxy groups at positions 11 and 21 and oxo groups at positions 3 and 20. Corticosterone is a 21-carbon steroid hormone of the corticosteroid type produced in the cortex of the adrenal glands.

name: formaldehyde
description: An aldehyde resulting from the formal oxidation of methanol.

name: choline alfoscerate
description: A member of the class of phosphocholines that is the choline ester of sn-glycero-3-phosphate. It is one of the major osmolyte in the renal medullary cells.

name: citrate(3-)
description: A tricarboxylic acid trianion, obtained by deprotonation of the three carboxy groups of citric acid.

name: cortisone
description: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is pregn-4-ene substituted by hydroxy groups at positions 17 and 21 and oxo group at positions 3, 11 and 20.

name: 11-deoxycorticosterone
description: A mineralocorticoid that is progesterone substituted at position 21 by a hydroxy group.

name: 5beta-androstane-3,17-dione
description: An androstane-3,17-dione with a 5β-configuration.

name: cholesteryl ester
description: A sterol ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of any carboxylic acid with the 3-hydroxy group of cholesterol.

name: progesterone
description: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid hormone in which a pregnane skeleton carries oxo substituents at positions 3 and 20 and is unsaturated at C(4)-C(5). As a hormone, it is involved in the female menstrual cycle, pregnancy and embryogenesis of humans and other species.

name: hexanoate
description: A short-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of hexanoic acid (also known as caproic acid).

name: nicotinamide
description: A pyridinecarboxamide that is pyridine in which the hydrogen at position 3 is replaced by a carboxamide group.

name: benzaldehyde
description: An arenecarbaldehyde that consists of benzene bearing a single formyl substituent; the simplest aromatic aldehyde and parent of the class of benzaldehydes.

name: 4-methylumbelliferone
description: A hydroxycoumarin that is umbelliferone substituted by a methyl group at position 4.

name: estrone
description: A 17-oxo steroid that is estra-1,3,5(10)-triene substituted by an hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 17.

name: myo-inositol
description: An inositol having myo- configuration.

name: all-trans-retinol
description: A retinol in which all four exocyclic double bonds have E- (trans-) geometry.

name: testosterone
description: An androstanoid having 17β-hydroxy and 3-oxo groups, together with unsaturation at C-4‒C-5..

name: D-glyceraldehyde
description: The <small>D</small>-enantiomer of glyceraldehyde.

name: 2-monoglyceride
description: A monoglyceride in which the acyl substituent is located at position 2.

name: aldehyde
description: A compound RC(=O)H, in which a carbonyl group is bonded to one hydrogen atom and to one R group.

name: enol-oxaloacetate
description: A C4-dicarboxylate resulting from the removal of a proton from both carboxylic acid groups of enol-oxaloacetic acid.

name: hydrogen donor
description: A molecular entity that can undergo oxidation by the loss of hydrogen atom(s).

name: hydrogencarbonate
description: The carbon oxoanion resulting from the removal of a proton from carbonic acid.

name: uracil
description: A common and naturally occurring pyrimidine nucleobase in which the pyrimidine ring is substituted with two oxo groups at positions 2 and 4. Found in RNA, it base pairs with adenine and replaces thymine during DNA transcription.

name: trans-hexadec-2-enal
description: A hexadecenal containing a double bond at position 2 (the trans-stereoisomer).

name: all-trans-retinyl palmitate
description: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of palmitic (hexadecanoic acid) with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol. It is used in cosmetic products to treat various skin disorders such as acne, skin aging, wrinkles, dark spots, and also protect against psoriasis.

name: nitrate
description: A nitrogen oxoanion formed by loss of a proton from nitric acid. Principal species present at pH 7.3.

name: 3-(indol-3-yl)pyruvate
description: A indol-3-yl carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 3-(indol-3-yl)pyruvic acid

name: cortisol
description: A 17α-hydroxy-C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is pregn-4-ene substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 20 and hydroxy groups at positions 11, 17 and 21. Cortisol is a corticosteroid hormone or glucocorticoid produced by zona fasciculata of the adrenal cortex, which is a part of the adrenal gland. It is usually referred to as the "stress hormone" as it is involved in response to stress and anxiety, controlled by corticotropin-releasing hormone (CRH). It increases blood pressure and blood sugar, and reduces immune responses.

name: L-thyroxine sulfate(1-)
description: A thyroxine sulfate(1−) that has <small>L</small>-configuration. The major microspecies at pH 7.3.

name: 9H-xanthine
description: An oxopurine in which the purine ring is substituted by oxo groups at positions 2 and 6 and N-9 is protonated.

name: desmosterol
description: A cholestanoid that is cholesta-5,24-diene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3. It is an intermediate metabolite obtained during the synthesis of cholesterol.

name: glycine betaine
description: The amino acid betaine derived from glycine.

name: glycerol
description: A triol with a structure of propane substituted at positions 1, 2 and 3 by hydroxy groups.

name: 4-methylumbelliferyl acetate
description: An acetate ester consiting of umbelliferone carrying a 7-O-acetyl group.

name: methanol
description: The primary alcohol that is the simplest aliphatic alcohol, comprising a methyl and an alcohol group.

name: organohalogen compound
description: A compound containing at least one carbon-halogen bond (where X is a halogen atom).

name: thymine
description: A pyrimidine nucleobase that is uracil in which the hydrogen at position 5 is replaced by a methyl group.

name: methylarsonous acid
description: A one-carbon compound that is arsonous acid in which the hydrogen attached to arsenic is replaced by a methyl group.

name: triglyceride
description: Any glyceride resulting from the condensation of all three hydroxy groups of glycerol (propane-1,2,3-triol) with fatty acids.

name: 4-methyl-2-oxopentanoate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 4-methyl-2-oxopentanoic acid.

name: all-trans-retinal
description: A retinal in which all four exocyclic double bonds have E- (trans-) geometry.

name: 7alpha-hydroxycholest-4-en-3-one
description: A cholestanoid consisting of a cholesterol core having an oxo group at the 3-position, a C=C bond at the 4,5-position and an α-hydroxy group at the 7-position.

name: calcidiol
description: A hydroxycalciol that is calciol in which the hydrogen at position 25 has been replaced by a hydroxy group. A prehormone resulting from the oxidation of calciol in the liver, it is further hydroxylated in the kidney to give calcitriol, the active form of vitamin D<small><sub>3</sub></small>.

name: butyrate
description: A short-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of butyric acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: chloride
description: A halide anion formed when chlorine picks up an electron to form an an anion.

name: keto-phenylpyruvate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion resulting from deprotonation of the carboxy group of keto-phenylpyruvic acid.

name: thiocyanate
description: A pseudohalide anion obtained by deprotonation of the thiol group of thiocyanic acid.

name: diglyceride
description: A glyceride that is glycerol in which any two of the hydroxy groups have been acylated. In the structure shown, two of the R groups (positions not specified) are acyl groups while the remaining R group can be either H or an alkyl group.

name: phylloquinone
description: A member of the class of phylloquinones that consists of 1,4-naphthoquinone having methyl and phytyl groups at positions 2 and 3 respectively. The parent of the class of phylloquinones.

name: 17,21-dihydroxy-5beta-pregnane-3,11,20-trione
description: A 4,5-dihydrocortisone that has β- configuration at position 5.

name: (R)-3,4-dihydroxymandelaldehyde
description: A 3,4-dihydroxymandelaldehyde that has R-configuration.

name: 3-phosphonatooxypyruvate(3-)
description: A carboxyalkyl phosphate oxoanion resuting from deprotonation of the carboxy and phosphate groups of 3-phosphooxypyruvic acid.

name: dodecanoate
description: A medium-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of dodecanoic acid (lauric acid); major species at pH 7.3.

name: alpha-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosylceramide
description: A glycotriaosylceramide having α-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-glucose as the trisaccharide component.

name: N-acyl-beta-D-galactosylsphingosine
description: An N-acyl-D-galactosylsphingosine in which the anomeric configuration of the galactosyl residue is β; sphingosine substituted at the O-1 position by a β-D-galactosyl group and at the N-2 position by an acyl group.

name: Arg-Phe(1+)
description: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy terminus and protonation of the side-chain guanidino group and the amino terminus of Arg-Phe; major species at pH 7.3.

name: L-Phe-L-Phe-Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
description: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of L-Phe-L-Phe-Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

name: Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
description: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

name: L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
description: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

name: goralatide(1-)
description: A peptide anion obtained by removal of protons from the two carboxy groups as well as protonation of the primary amino group of goralatide; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-acetyl-L-seryl-L-aspartate(2-)
description: A peptide anion obtained by deprotonation of the two carboxy groups of N-acetyl-L-seryl-L-aspartic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: Lys-Pro(1+)
description: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy terminus and protonation of the side-chain amino group and the amino terminus of Lys-Pro; major species at pH 7.3.

name: all-trans-13,14-dihydroretinoate
description: A retinoid anion that is the conjugate base of all-trans-13,14-dihydroretinoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 15-deoxy-Delta(12,14)-prostaglandin J2-S-glutathione conjugate(2-)
description: A prostanoid anion obtained by deprotonation of the three carboxy groups and protonation of the glutamyl α-amino group of 15-deoxy-Δ12,14-prostaglandin J2-S-glutathione conjugate; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 1D-myo-inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate(6-)
description: Hexaanion of 1D-myo-inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate arising from glabal deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5beta-dihydrotestosterone
description: A 17β-hydroxyandrostan-3-one that has β- configuration at position 5. It is a metabolite of testosterone.

name: 2-phenylethanaminium
description: The cation obtained by protonation of the amino group of 2-phenylethylamine.

name: benzylaminium
description: The conjugate acid of benzylamine; major product at pH 7.3.

name: beta-D-glucosyl-N-acylsphingosine
description: A D-glucosyl-N-acylsphingosine in which the glucosyl head group has β-configuration at the anomeric centre.

name: 7alpha,12alpha-dihydroxy-5beta-cholestan-3-one
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestan-3-one bearing two additional hydroxy substituents at positions 7α and 12α.

name: 7alpha-hydroxy-5beta-cholestan-3-one
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestan-3-one bearing an additional hydroxy substituent at position 7α .

name: N(6)-[(S)-lactoyl]-L-lysine residue
description: An N6-acyl-L-lysine residue that has (S)-lactoyl as the N6-acyl group.

name: ribonucleotidyl-2'-3'-cyclophospho-GMP(2-)
description: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any ribonucleotidyl-2'-3'-cyclophospho-GMP; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5'-end 5'-hydroxy ribonucleotidyl-ribonucleotide(1-)
description: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any 5'-hydroxy-ribonucleotidyl-ribonucleotide residue.

name: 5'-end 5'-hydroxy-deoxyribonucleotidyl-deoxyribonucleotide(1-) residue
description: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any 5'-end 5'-hydroxy-deoxyribonucleotidyl-deoxyribonucleotide residue; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (R,S)-glycero-3-phospho-(3'-acyl-1'-glycerol)(1-)
description: An anionic phospholipid obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any (R,S)-glycero-3-phospho-(3'-acyl-1'-glycerol); major species at pH 7.3.

name: (R,S)-glycero-1-phospho-(3'-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-1'-glycerol)(1-)
description: An anionic phospholipid obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of (R,S)-glycero-1-phospho-(3'-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-1'-glycerol); major species at pH 7.3.

name: 11-oxo-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
description: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional oxo substituent at position 11.

name: 11-keto-5alpha-androstane-3alpha,17beta-diol
description: An androstanoid that is 5α-androstane carrying an oxo group at position 11 and two hydroxy groups at the 3α and 17β positions.

name: behenate
description: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of behenic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: adrenosterone
description: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is androst-4-ene carrying three oxo-substituents at positions 3, 11 and 17.

name: octadecanoate
description: A fatty acid anion 18:0 that is the conjugate base of octadecanoic acid (stearic acid). Stearates have a variety of uses in the pharmaceutical industry.

name: octanoate
description: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of octanoic acid (caprylic acid); believed to block adipogenesis.

name: anandamide
description: An N-acylethanolamine 20:4 resulting from the formal condensation of carboxy group of arachidonic acid with the amino group of ethanolamine.

name: 5,6-dihydrothymine
description: A pyrimidone obtained by formal addition of hydrogen across the 5,6-position of thymine.

name: aldosterone
description: A pregnane-based steroidal hormone produced by the outer-section (zona glomerulosa) of the adrenal cortex in the adrenal gland, and acts on the distal tubules and collecting ducts of the kidney to cause the conservation of sodium, secretion of potassium, increased water retention, and increased blood pressure. The overall effect of aldosterone is to increase reabsorption of ions and water in the kidney.

name: decanoate
description: A fatty acid anion 10:0 that is the conjugate base of decanoic acid.

name: 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetaldehyde
description: A phenylacetaldehyde in which the 3 and 4 positions of the phenyl group are substituted by hydroxy groups.

name: 24,25-dihydrolanosterol
description: A 3β-sterol formed from lanosterol by reduction across the C-24‒C-25 double bond.

name: 1-O-oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: An oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group at position 1 is (9Z)-octadecenoyl (oleoyl) and the hydroxy group at position 2 is unsubstituted.

name: 2-oxopentanoate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxopentanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: dehydroepiandrosterone
description: An androstanoid that is androst-5-ene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 17. It is a naturally occurring steroid hormone produced by the adrenal glands.

name: fatty acid anion
description: The conjugate base of a fatty acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group of the corresponding fatty acid.

name: ammonium
description: An onium cation obtained by protonation of ammonia.

name: calciol
description: A hydroxy seco-steroid that is (5Z,7E)-9,10-secocholesta-5,7,10(19)-triene in which the pro-S hydrogen at position 3 has been replaced by a hydroxy group. It is the inactive form of vitamin D3, being hydroxylated in the liver to calcidiol (25-hydroxyvitamin D3), which is then further hydroxylated in the kidney to give calcitriol (1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3), the active hormone.

name: 5alpha-pregnane-3,20-dione
description: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid hormone that is 5α-pregnane substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 20. It is a metabolite of progestrone.

name: manganese(2+)
description: A divalent metal cation in which the metal is manganese.

name: copper(2+)
description: An ion of copper carrying a double positive charge.

name: sodium(1+)
description: A monoatomic monocation obtained from sodium.

name: potassium(1+)
description: A monoatomic monocation obtained from potassium.

name: thiol
description: An organosulfur compound in which a thiol group, ‒SH, is attached to a carbon atom of any aliphatic or aromatic moiety.

name: lithocholate
description: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of lithocholic acid.

name: glycocholate
description: A cholanic acid conjugate anion that is the conjugate base of glycocholic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: cholate
description: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of cholic acid.

name: fumarate(2-)
description: A C4-dicarboxylate that is the E-isomer of but-2-enedioate(2−)

name: L-glutamate(1-)
description: An α-amino-acid anion that is the conjugate base of <small>L</small>-glutamic acid, having anionic carboxy groups and a cationic amino group

name: D-glutamate(1-)
description: An α-amino-acid anion that is the conjugate base of <small>D</small>-glutamic acid, having anionic carboxy groups and a cationic amino group

name: D-aspartate(1-)
description: An aspartate(1−) that is the conjugate base of <small>D</small>-aspartic acid.

name: L-aspartate(1-)
description: An aspartate(1−) that is the  conjugate base of <small>L</small>-aspartic acid.

name: L-serine residue
description: A serine residue that has <small>L</small> configuration.

name: succinate(2-)
description: A dicarboxylic acid dianion resulting from the removal of a proton from both of the carboxy groups of succinic acid.

name: acetate
description: A monocarboxylic acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of acetic acid.

name: 5beta-pregnane-3,20-dione
description: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is 5β-pregnane with oxo groups at positions 3 and 20.

name: linoleate
description: An octadecadienoate with cis- double bonds at the 9- and 12- positions; the conjugate base of linoleic acid.

name: heme
description: A heme is any tetrapyrrolic chelate of iron.

name: ATP(4-)
description: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of ATP; major species present at pH 7.3.

name: maleate(2-)
description: A C4-dicarboxylate that is the Z-isomer of but-2-enedioate(2−)

name: tetradecanoate
description: A long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of myristic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: oleate
description: A C<small><sub>18</sub></small>, long straight-chain monounsaturated fatty acid anion; and the conjugate base of oleic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: elaidate
description: A long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of elaidic acid; shown to exert detrimental effects on mitochondrial lipid composition and function.

name: alcohol
description: A compound in which a hydroxy group, ‒OH, is attached to a saturated carbon atom.

name: 1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A glycerophosphocholine that consists of <small>L</small>-α-glycerophosphocholine carrying an unspecified alkyl substituent at position 1.

name: tetracosanoate
description: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of tetracosanoic acid (lignoceric acid), formed by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

name: N-acylsphinganine
description: A ceramide consisting of sphinganine in which one of the amino hydrogens is substituted by a fatty acyl group.

name: icosanoate
description: A long-chain fatty acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of icosanoic acid (arachidic acid).

name: palmitoleate
description: A hexadec-9-enoate that is the conjugate base of palmitoleic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: alpha-linolenate
description: A linolenate that is the conjugate base of α-linolenic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: (15Z)-tetracosenoate
description: A tetracosenoate that is the conjugate base of nervonic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: arachidonate
description: A long-chain fatty acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of arachidonic acid.

name: gondoate
description: A very long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of gondoic acid, formed by deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: L-lysinium(1+)
description: An optically active form of lysinium having <small>L</small>-configuration.

name: L-argininium(1+)
description: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of argininium(1+).

name: tyraminium
description: An ammonium ion that is the conjugate acid of tyramine; major species at pH 7.3.

name: epoxide
description: Any cyclic ether in which the oxygen atom forms part of a 3-membered ring.

name: beta-D-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate(4-)
description: A <small>D</small>-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate(4−) that is the conjugate base of β-<small>D</small>-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate.

name: diphosphate(3-)
description: A trivalent inorganic anion obtained by removal of all three protons from diphosphoric acid.

name: carboxylic ester
description: An ester of a carboxylic acid, R<small><sup>1</small></sup>C(=O)OR<small><sup>2</small></sup>, where R<small><sup>1</small></sup> = H or organyl and R<small><sup>2</small></sup> = organyl.

name: L-serine zwitterion
description: A serine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of <small>L</small>-serine.

name: phenols
description: Organic aromatic compounds having one or more hydroxy groups attached to a benzene or other arene ring.

name: 1-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
description: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol where arachidonoyl forms the 1-acyl group.

name: 11-oxotestosterone
description: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is testosterone carrying an additional oxo substituent at position 11.

name: (24S)-24-hydroxycholesterol
description: A 24-hydroxycholesterol that has S configuration at position 24. It is the major metabolic breakdown product of cholesterol in the brain.

name: boldenone
description: An  3-oxo-Δ<small><sup>1</small></sup>,Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid substituted by an oxo group at position 3 and a β-hydroxy group at position 17. It is an  anabolic androgenic steroid that has been developed for veterinary use.

name: tributyrin
description: A triglyceride obtained by formal acylation of the three hydroxy groups of glycerol by butyric acid.

name: 2-oxohexanoate
description: A medium-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxohexanoic acid.

name: D-serine zwitterion
description: A serine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of <small>D</small>-serine.

name: acylcholine
description: A choline ester formed from choline and a carboxylic acid.

name: all-trans-retinoate
description: A retinoate that is the conjugate base of all-trans-retinoic acid.

name: 17beta-hydroxy steroid
description: A 17-hydroxy steroid in which the hydroxy group at position 17 has a β-configuration.

name: 1-monoglyceride
description: A monoglyceride in which the acyl substituent is located at position 1.

name: crotonate
description: The conjugate base of crotonic acid; used by some bacterial species as a carbon and energy source.

name: chenodeoxycholate
description: Conjugate base of chenodeoxycholic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)pyruvate
description: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion obtained by removal of a proton from the carboxylic acid group of 3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)pyruvic acid.

name: glycochenodeoxycholate
description: A N-acylglycinate that is the conjugate base of glycochenodeoxycholic acid.

name: taurocholate
description: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurocholic acid.

name: taurodeoxycholate
description: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurodeoxycholic acid.

name: galactosylceramide
description: Any of the cerebrosides in which the monosaccharide head group is galactose.

name: glyoxylate
description: The conjugate base of glyoxylic acid.

name: 2-acetyl-1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
description: A 2-acyl-1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine where the 2-O-acyl group is specified as acetyl; a potent phospholipid activator and mediator of many leukocyte functions, including platelet aggregation, inflammation, and anaphylaxis.

name: 5alpha-androstane-3alpha,17beta-diol
description: The 5α-stereoisomer of androstane-3α,17β-diol.

name: 3beta-hydroxy steroid
description: A 3-hydroxy steroid in which the 3-hydroxy substituent is in the  β-position.

name: phytanate
description: A branched-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of phytanic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

name: CTP(4-)
description: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of CTP; major species present at pH 7.3.

name: GTP(4-)
description: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of GTP; major species present at pH 7.3.

name: dTTP(4-)
description: A 2'-deoxyribonucleoside 5'-triphosphate(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the four triphosphate OH groups of dTTP; major species at pH 7.3.

name: thiamine(1+) monophosphate(2-)
description: An organophosphate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of thiamine(1+) monophosphate(1−), arising from the deprotonation of the phosphate group. Major microspecies at pH 7.3.

name: D-fructofuranose
description: A fructofuranose that has <small>D</small> configuration.

name: L-ascorbate
description: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of ascorbate and conjugate base of <small>L</small>-ascorbic acid, arising from selective deprotonation of the 3-hydroxy group. Required for a range of essential metabolic reactions in all animals and plants.

name: cholesteryl linoleate
description: The (9Z,12Z)-stereoisomer of cholesteryl octadeca-9,12-dienoate.

name: D-glucopyranose
description: A glucopyranose having <small>D</small>-configuration.

name: 1,2-dioctadecanoyl-sn-glycerol
description: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol where both acyl groups are specified as octadecanoyl.

name: epitestosterone
description: An androstanoid that is the C-17 epimer of testosterone.

name: hydrogenphosphate
description: A phosphate ion that is the conjugate base of dihydrogenphosphate.

name: 2-O-acetyl-1-O-hexadecyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine

name: 2-arachidonoylglycerol

name: N-acylsphingosine

name: N-hexacosanoylsphinganine

name: prostaglandin G2 2-glyceryl ester
description: A 2-monoglyceride obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin G<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 2-hydroxy group of glycerol.

name: prostaglandin H2 2-glyceryl ester
description: A 2-monoglyceride obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin H<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 2-hydroxy group of glycerol.

name: 15-deoxy-Delta(12,14)-prostaglandin J2(1-)
description: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 15-deoxy-Δ<small><sup>12,14</small></sup>-prostaglandin J<small><sub>2</sub></small>. obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N-dodecanoylsphinganine
description: A dihydroceramide in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as dodecanoyl (lauroyl).

name: N-(dodecanoyl)ethanolamine
description: An N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of dodecanoic acid (myristic acid) with the amino group of ethanolamine.

name: N-(octadecanoyl)ethanolamine
description: An N-acylethanolamine 18:0 that is the ethanolamide of octadecanoic acid.

name: L-3-oxoalanine residue
description: An <small>L</small>-α-amino acid residue derived from <small>L</small>-3-oxoalanine.

name: 4beta-hydroxycholesterol
description: A oxysterol that is cholesterol in which the hydrogen at the 4β position has been replaced by a hydroxy group. A metabolite of cholesterol formed by the drug-metabolizing enzyme cytochrome P450 3A4, it is one of the major oxysterols in the human circulation.

name: (25R)-3beta,4beta-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-al
description: A 26-oxo steroid resulting from the oxidation of the terminal methyl group of 4β,26-dihydroxycholesterol to the corresponding aldehyde.

name: (25R)-3beta,4beta-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-oate(1-)
description: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of (25R)-3β,4β-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-oic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5beta-dihydroepitestosterone
description: An androstanoid with a 5β-configuration formed by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond of epitestosterone.

name: 5beta-dihydrocorticosterone
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from corticosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

name: 5beta-dihydrodeoxycorticosterone
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from 11-deoxycorticosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

name: 5beta-dihydroaldosterone
description: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from aldosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

name: (5alpha)-cholestan-3beta-ol
description: A cholestanoid that is (5α)-cholestane substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3.

name: O-(N-acetyl-alpha-D-galactosaminyl)-L-threonine residue
description: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from O-(N-acetyl-α-D-galactosaminyl)-L-threonine.

name: 17beta-hydroxy-5beta-androst-1-en-3-one
description: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>1</small></sup>-steroid with 5β-configuration formed from 17β-hydroxyandrosta-1,4-dien-3-one by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

name: 17beta-hydroxy-5beta-estran-3-one
description: A C18 steroid with 5β-configuration formed from nandrolone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

name: (25R)-3beta,26-dihydroxycholest-5-en-7-one
description: An oxysterol that is 7-ketocholesterol which is substituted by a hydroxy group at position 26 and has R-configuration at position 25.

name: (25R)-3beta-hydroxycholest-5-en-7-one-26-oate
description: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of (25R)-3β-hydroxycholest-5-en-7-one-26-oic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (S)-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA(4-)
description: A 2-methylbutanoyl-CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of (S)-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

name: N(omega),N('omega)-dimethyl-L-arginine(1+) residue
description: An α-amino-acid residue cation obtained by protonation of the guanidino group of Nω,N'ω-dimethyl-L-arginine residue.

name: prostaglandin E2 1-glyceryl ester
description: A 1-monoglyceride resulting from the condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin E<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 1-hydroxy group of glycerol.

name: prostaglandin F2alpha 1-glyceryl ester
description: A 1-monoglyceride resulting from the condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin F<small><sub>2α</sub></small> with the 1-hydroxy group of glycerol.

name: L-isoaspartate alpha-methyl ester residue
description: An amino acid residue derived from <small>L</small>-isoaspartic acid α-methyl ester.

name: 5(S)-HETE(1-)
description: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S)-HETE, obtained by deprotonation of the hydroxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 12(S)-HHTrE(1-)
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 12(S)-HHTrE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 12-HETE(1-)
description: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 12-HETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

name: prostaglandin H1(1-)
description: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin H<small><sub>1</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 5(S),15(R)-DiHETE(1-)
description: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S),15(R)-DiHETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 17(R)-HDoHE(1-)
description: A hydroxy polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 17(R)-HDoHE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (4Z,7Z,10Z,14E,16Z,19Z)-13-hydroxydocosahexaenoate
description: A hydroxy polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 13-HDoHE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 18(R)-HEPE(1-)
description: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 18(R)-HEPE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: 15(R)-HEPE(1-)
description: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 15(R)-HEPE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

name: (7Z,10Z,13R,14E,16Z,19Z)-13-hydroxydocosapentaenoate
description: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (13R)-hydroxy-(7Z,10Z,14E,16Z,19Z)-docosapentaenoic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3. It is an intermediate in 13-series resolvins biosynthesis from DPA (ω-3).

name: taurochenodeoxycholate
description: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurochenodeoxycholic acid arising from deprotonation of the sulfonate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.
